---
title: "03a Probability, Statistics, And State Estimation"
format: html
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Purpose

This submodule connects the visual-localization course to the probability and filtering foundations behind the estimator.

The material is provided through Roger R. Labbe's _Kalman and Bayesian Filters in Python_ as a Git submodule under:

```text
external/kalman-bayesian-filters/
```

## Recommended Reading Path

| Course Need | Kalman Notebook |
|---|---|
| Probability updates and belief state | [02 Discrete Bayes](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/02-Discrete-Bayes.ipynb) |
| Gaussian uncertainty and measurement noise | [03 Gaussians](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/03-Gaussians.ipynb) |
| First Kalman filter intuition | [04 One-Dimensional Kalman Filters](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/04-One-Dimensional-Kalman-Filters.ipynb) |
| Multivariate state vectors | [06 Multivariate Kalman Filters](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/06-Multivariate-Kalman-Filters.ipynb) |
| Deriving the Kalman equations | [07 Kalman Filter Math](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/07-Kalman-Filter-Math.ipynb) |
| Engineering filter matrices | [08 Designing Kalman Filters](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/08-Designing-Kalman-Filters.ipynb) |
| Nonlinear localization intuition | [11 Extended Kalman Filters](../generated/kalman-bayesian-filters/11-Extended-Kalman-Filters.ipynb) |

## Setup

Clone the course with submodules:

```bash
git clone --recurse-submodules <repo-url>
```

If the repo is already cloned:

```bash
git submodule update --init --recursive
```

Install optional dependencies for running the Kalman notebooks interactively:

```bash
uv sync --dev --group state-estimation
```

## How It Fits IPCV

The IPCV localization module implements a small constant-velocity Kalman estimator for the production-system lessons. The external notebooks explain the probability, Gaussian, and Kalman-filter theory that justifies the estimator.

Use the external notebooks for conceptual depth. Use `ipcv.localization`, `localize.py`, configs, tests, and MLflow artifacts for the production engineering workflow.

## Exercise

Read `04-One-Dimensional-Kalman-Filters`, then return to `ipcv.localization.KalmanLocalizationEstimator` and identify which code lines correspond to prediction, innovation, Kalman gain, and correction.
